question: What is the base-10 logarithm of the atomic oxygen erosion yield of the polymer Polyurethane [Dureflex PS8010] represented by SMILES [*]Nc1ccc(Cc2ccc(NC(=O)OCCOC([*])=O)cc2)cc1, with no coating, with a film thickness of 0.0508 mm, oriented in the ram direction for a mission time of 4.0 years, subjected to a solar exposure of 6300 equivalent sun hours and an atomic oxygen fluence of 8.430e+21 atom/cm^2?

What is the base-10 logarithm of the atomic oxygen erosion yield of the polymer BTDA-BENZIDENE represented by SMILES [*]C(=O)c1ccc2c(c1)C(=O)N(c1ccc(-c3ccc(N4C(=O)c5ccc([*])cc5C4=O)cc3)cc1)C2=O, with no coating, with a film thickness of 0.035 mm, oriented in the ram direction for a mission time of 0.00476598 years, subjected to a solar exposure of 20 equivalent sun hours and an atomic oxygen fluence of 3.500e+20 atom/cm^2?
